attributes.content:
## Overview

The Calendar card gives the workspace a structured home for calendar container: named collection of calendar events with view modes (day/week/month).

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Calendar acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Task]]** -- Calendar often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Alert]]** -- Alert can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Calendar.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Calendar can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Document]]** -- Calendar can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Recipe]]** -- Recipe can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Calendar.
- **[[Timer]]** -- Timer can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Calendar.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Calendar as the structured home for calendar container: named collection of calendar events with view modes (day/week/month) and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Calendar when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Calendar stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Calendar
cardInfo.summary: Calendar container: named collection of calendar events with view modes (day/week/month).
